rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just during the administration of the estate. (3) Administrator alleges that she took no actions under the IAEA, but she sold all of the securities. Notwithstanding the waivers of accounting, the net proceeds of the sale should be set forth in the report. [CRC 7.550(b).] (4) In addition to the requirements of CRC 7.651, the petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.] (5) Administrator is holding assets in a financial institution outside of California. [Probate Code Section 9700.] (6) Is $281,639.67 the amount before deducting fees and reserve or after? What is the ending date of the reporting period (the date as of which this amount was on hand)? (7) The supplement filed on 10/26/23 was e-filed when a Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit was attached to it. These must be conventionally filed. [LRSF 14.59 C.2.] (8) The Letters issued to James Gleneck and to Alyssa Flores were certified in 2021, not within 60 days. [LRSF 14.35 G.] (9) It is unclear if Amy Gleneck's share is to be distributed to the administrator of her estate or to her heirs. Since her husband was reportedly appointed as the personal representative of her estate, the distribution should presumably be made to him. (10) No agreement for the division of the statutory compensation to the personal representatives has been filed. The proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no need to attach a copy of the petition to the proof of service of notice. Shirley Yawitz was the probate referee appointed in the order dated 6/17/19 and confirmed as such in the order dated 11/23/21. Letters were issued to Amy Gleneck on 8/29/19, not 6/18/19. She was appointed with limited IAEA authority on 6/17/19, not 8/29/19. California abolished the estate tax for decedents dying on or after 1/1/2005. $281,639.67 is exact, not approximate.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICK DAVID LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The estate cash on hand in excess of $805,000 has been held at a single financial institution and has been uninsured since March 2023 when the estate sold its real property. The administrator has not taken any action to protect the uninsured portion of the estate cash since the issue was raised before the initial hearing on 10/24/2023. 2) The administrator's attorneys request $8,765 for their extraordinary services related to the sale of the estate real property. Statutory fees payable to the attorneys for their ordinary services amount to $29,315.96. The attorneys' supporting time records show $20,315 billed for ordinary services actually rendered to the estate. See CRC 7.703(a) allowing the Court to consider the amount of statutory fees for ordinary services when determining additional fees for extraordinary services. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF GEORGINA LAXMI LOREK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Create Trust, Transfer Funds, And Terminate Guardianship Estate; Authority To Pay Child Care And Camp Expenses From Guardianship Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The guardian-parent requests reimbursement out of the estate for child care and summer camp expenses for both minors. The Court is to review the guardian-parent's income and expense declaration on file and determine whether good cause exists under Probate Code section 2422. 2) The guardian requests an order authorizing him to create a trust for each minor and terminating this guardianship. The memorandum of points and authorities filed on 11/22/2023 did not address the following issues raised by the Court's tentative ruling for the last hearing: Section 3611(g), cited in the petition as statutory authority to create a minor's trust, only applies when there is no guardianship estate. See section 3610. Section 3602 (presumably subdivision(c)(3)), also cited in the petition as statutory authority to create a minor's trust, only allows the "court giving the judgment for a minor described in section 3600" to create or approve the trust, but the order for final distribution issued in the related probate case does not appear to qualify as the "judgment" defined in section 3600. See Estate of Kampen (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 971, 986-988 ("It is not a judgment that requires the payment of money. Rather, it is a judgment distributing assets. The distribution order settles all claims involving all the assets in the estate, and is clearly not a money judgment in favor of one particular party"). If the order for final distribution should be treated as the section 3600 judgment, the request to create a trust for each minor had to be made in the related probate case before the Court issued the final distribution order, based on the statutory language of section 3602(c)(3) ("court giving the judgment referred to in section 3600" and "trust which is either created by, or approved of, in the judgment described in section 3600"). The judgment (order for final distribution) has become final and conclusive, as it has not been appealed or set aside to add a provision creating or approving a distributee trust for each minor. Even if the belated request to create a minor's trust should be enteratined, it should be noted that: in the event of either minor's death, the guardian would be the sole beneficiary as the minor's sole heir; and if the trust is placed under the Court's supervision, the trustee is subject to the same terms and conditions as the guardian of the estate, and prior court approval is required for many acts of the trustee including distribution from the trust to pay the minors' expenses. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET A. FOOTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No will or codicil, original or lost, has been LODGED with the Court. [Probate Code Section 8200.] (2) A petition cannot be amended by a "Declaration." [CRC 7.3.] (3) An amendment requires the same notice (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (4) Nothing has been filed to overcome the presumption of revocation. [LRSF 14.20.] After the original or lost will and codicil are LODGED with the clerk, a fully amended Judicial Council form Amended Petition for Probate of LOST Will, etc., must be filed and noticed for the 2/6/24 hearing. Deadline is 1/26/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. If the will and codicil are lost, a copy of the lost testamentary documents must be attached to the proposed order. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Whenever issues are raised by an examiner, they should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) not in a "Declaration to Respond to Examiner's Notes," which only serves to highlight defects. Nibling is a gender-neutral term used to refer to a child of one's sibling as a replacement for "niece" or "nephew".)

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed since the last hearing on 11/14/2023: 1) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide a missing telephone number and day of signing. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) No proof of publication is on file. 4) The petitioner's name stated at items 2 and 3 of the DE-121 form must exactly match the name under which this petition was filed (no middle initial). 5) The petition caption: The first box must be checked, as the petitioner is named as executor in the will. 6) Item 2b(1) must be checked. 7) The proof of service attached to the DE-121 form does not show direct notice given to Barbara Meyer. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). No waiver of direct notice signed by her or her attorney is on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SANDRA J. ZRNIC LIVING TRUST DATED JULY 27, 2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of A Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/02/2024 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-115 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. 3) If no probate administration is pending or anticipated for the settlor's estate, all heirs of the settlor (who would have standing to contest the pour-over will and object to this petition) must be identified (names and addresses) by a verified supplement. Those heirs must be served in the manner required by Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to service of a civil summons. A family tree showing names and dates of death of all predeceased heirs and their relationships to the surviving heirs must also be included in the supplement. 4) If the settlor did not survive her spouse for more than 15 years, the predeceased spouse's heirs under section 6402.5(a) must be identified (names and addresses) by a verified supplement. The predeceased spouse's heirs must be served in the same manner as the settlor's heirs. A family tree showing names and dates of death of all predeceased heirs and their relationships to the predeceased spouse must also be included in the supplement. 5) How did the settlor and her predeceased spouse hold title? Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument showing how they held title at the time of the predeceased spouse's death. 6) Exhibit B (grant deed) shows a transfer of title from the settlor individually to herself as trustee. Please explain why the Court's order confirming title is necessary. Note: If the settlor and her predeceased spouse are alleged to have left no next of kin, a declaration describing efforts to ascertain their next of kin must be filed. Then the state must be served through the Attorney General as the heir. See sections 44, 56, 1209, 6404, and 6800.
